title: bouteloua hirsuta subsp. pectinata
intro: Bouteloua hirsuta subsp. pectinata, also known as the hairy grama grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Bouteloua hirsuta subsp. pectinata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Bouteloua hirsuta subsp. pectinata can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams to utilize its medicinal properties.
